concepts.title: What is Science?
concepts.definition: Science is a way of thinking, observing and doing things to understand the world we live in and to uncover the secrets of the universe. It is about asking questions, exploring the world and trying to understand how things work.
examples: Studying tiny grains of sand or massive mountains
Looking at stars in the night sky

concepts.title: Science Around Us
concepts.definition: Science is everywhere - from the depths of the ocean to the vastness of outer space, from the kitchen to the playground. Every aspect of our daily life involves scientific principles.

concepts.title: Variety in Living Organisms
concepts.definition: The living world shows immense diversity in forms, sizes, colors, and habitats. Different organisms have different characteristics that help them survive in their environments.
examples: Different types of plants - trees, shrubs, herbs

concepts.title: Classification of Living Things
concepts.definition: Living organisms can be classified into different groups based on their characteristics. This helps us study and understand them better.

chapters.title: Mindful Eating: A Path to a Healthy Body

concepts.title: Food and Nutrition
concepts.definition: Food provides us with energy and nutrients necessary for growth, development, and maintaining health. Different foods contain different nutrients.

concepts.title: Balanced Diet
concepts.definition: A balanced diet contains all the nutrients in proper proportions required for healthy living. It includes carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, and water.
examples: Including all food groups in meals
Eating variety of fruits and vegetables

concepts.title: Properties of Magnets
concepts.definition: Magnets are objects that attract certain materials like iron. Every magnet has two poles - North pole and South pole. Like poles repel and unlike poles attract each other.

concepts.title: Uses of Magnets
concepts.definition: Magnets have many practical applications in our daily life and in various devices.

concepts.title: Measurement of Length
concepts.definition: Length is the measurement of distance between two points. We use different units to measure length depending on the distance - millimeter, centimeter, meter, kilometer.
examples: Using ruler to measure small objects

concepts.title: Motion
concepts.definition: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time. An object is in motion if its position changes with time.

concepts.title: Types of Materials
concepts.definition: Materials are substances from which objects are made. Materials can be classified based on their properties like appearance, hardness, solubility, transparency, etc.

concepts.title: Properties of Materials
concepts.definition: Different materials have different properties which make them suitable for different uses. Properties include hardness, transparency, solubility, and flexibility.
examples: Glass is transparent - used for windows
Iron is hard - used for construction
Plastic is flexible - used for bags

concepts.title: What is Temperature?
concepts.definition: Temperature is the measure of hotness or coldness of an object or substance. It is measured using a thermometer.

concepts.title: Measurement of Temperature
concepts.definition: Temperature is measured using thermometers. The common scales are Celsius (°C) and Fahrenheit (°F). Clinical thermometer is used to measure body temperature.

chapters.title: A Journey through States of Water

concepts.title: Three States of Water
concepts.definition: Water exists in three states - solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (water vapor). Water can change from one state to another by heating or cooling.
examples: Water vapor condensing to water droplets

concepts.title: Water Cycle
concepts.definition: The water cycle is the continuous movement of water between earth and atmosphere through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation.

chapters.title: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

concepts.title: Need for Separation
concepts.definition: Separation of substances is required to remove unwanted materials, obtain pure substances, or separate useful components from a mixture.

concepts.title: Methods of Separation
concepts.definition: Different methods are used to separate mixtures based on the properties of components - handpicking, winnowing, sieving, sedimentation, decantation, filtration, evaporation.
examples: Winnowing to separate grain from husk
Evaporation to obtain salt from seawater

concepts.title: Characteristics of Living Things
concepts.definition: Living organisms have certain characteristics that distinguish them from non-living things - growth, movement, reproduction, respiration, excretion, and response to stimuli.
examples: Animals moving in search of food

concepts.title: Habitats of Living Creatures
concepts.definition: Habitat is the natural environment where an organism lives. Different organisms live in different habitats based on their adaptations.

concepts.title: Natural Resources
concepts.definition: Natural resources are materials and substances that occur naturally and are useful to humans. They include air, water, soil, minerals, forests, and wildlife.

concepts.title: Conservation of Resources
concepts.definition: Conservation means using natural resources wisely and protecting them for future generations. We should avoid wastage and pollution.
examples: Save water by fixing leaky taps

concepts.title: The Solar System
concepts.definition: The Solar System consists of the Sun and all objects that orbit around it, including eight planets, their moons, asteroids, and comets.
examples: Eight planets - Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune

concepts.title: Stars and Galaxies
concepts.definition: Stars are huge balls of hot, glowing gases. Our Sun is a star. Billions of stars together form a galaxy. Our galaxy is called the Milky Way.
